title: Nascent RNA 4sU labelling and enrichment
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"uicjx","text":"4sU-seq allows for measuring or nascent transcription which can reflect dynamic changes more accurately than traditional RNA-seq. Incorporation of the nucleoside analog 4-thiouridine into RNA allows the separation of nascent transcripts though reversible coupling to biotin and streptavdin bead pull-down. The protocol takes roughly two days to generate a sequencing ready library.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"s5awc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"75o39","text":"4sU-seq allows for measuring or nascent transcription which can reflect dynamic expression changes more accurately than traditional RNA-seq. Incorporation of the nucleoside analog 4-thiouridine into RNA allows the separation of nascent transcripts though reversible coupling to biotin and streptavdin bead pull-down1-4. steps: Prepare 10 5 cells per mL of media
Add 10 µL of 500 mM 4‐thiouridine (4sU) per mL of cells for a final concentration of 500 μM.
Incubate under standard growth conditions for 45 minutes (e.g. 37°C, 5% CO 2 )
Pellet the cells by centrifugation at 200 rcf for 5 min.
Discard supernatant and resuspend cells in an equal volume of PBS.
Pellet the cells by centrifugation at 200 rcf for 5 min.
Resuspend pellet in 1 mL of TRI reagent and transfer to microcentrifuge tube.
Snap freeze cells using liquid nitrogen OR dry ice and ethanol. Store lysed cells at -80ºC for up to 3 months
Chill centrifuge to 4ºC
Thaw TRI reagent lysed cells on ice.
Add 200 μL chloroform.
Add 1 μL glycoblue and close tube securely.
Shake the tube vigorously for 15 sec.
Incubate at room temperaturefor for 2–3 min.
Centrifuge for 30 min at 12,000 rcf (4°C).
Transfer the upper aqueous phase to a new collection tube containing 500μL isopropanol.
Incubate for 10 min at room temperature.
Centrifuge for 10 min at 12,000 rcf (4°C).
Discard supernatant and wash the RNA pellet with 700 µL 70% ethanol in DEPC-treated water.
Centrifuge for 10 min at 12,000 rcf (4°C).
Discard supernatant and air dry for 10 min.
Re-suspend in 20 µL DEPC-treated water.
Measure RNA concentration using a Nanodrop.
Pre-spin one phase-lock tube per sample Heat 2 mL MACS wash buffer to 65°C
Combine 20-100 μg of total RNA, 50 μL MTSEA-biotin-XX, 25 µL 10x Biotinylation buffer and sufficient DEPC-treated water to generate a 250 μL reaction.
Incubate for 30 min at room temperature with rotation.
Add 400 μL chloroform/isoamylalcohol (24:1).
Add 1 μL glycoblue.
Mix and incubate for 3 min.
Transfer mixture to a phase-lock tube.
Centrifuge for 5 min at 12,000 rcf (room temp).
Transfer upper phase (~230 µL) to a new tube.
Add 1:10 volume 5 M NaCl and an equal volume of isopropanol and mix.
Centrifuge for 20 min at 20,000 rcf (4ºC).
Discared supernatant and wash pellet with 500 µL of 75% ethanol in DEPC-treated water.
Centrifuge for 20 min at 20,000 rcf (4ºC).
Discared supernatant and resuspend pellet in 60μl DEPC-treated water.
Denature RNA at 65°C for 10 min followed by rapid cooling on ice for 5 min.
Incubate RNA with 60 μL streptavidin magnetic beads for 15 min at room temperature with rotation.
Place a µMACS column into a magnetic separator with a 15 mL waste collection tube.
Add the biotinylation reaction to the coulmn.
Wash the column with 1 mL 65ºC MACS was buffer, repeat for a total of two washes.
Wash the column with 1 mL room temp MACS was buffer, repeat for a total of two washes.
Replace the waste tube with a collection microcentrifuge tube.
Elute RNA from the column by adding 100 μL of freshly prepared 100 mM dithiothreitol (DTT), followed by a second elution with another 100 µL 5 min later.
Clean up labelled RNA using a Qiagen RNeasy RNA clean-up kit with on-column DNase digestion.
Elute purified RNA in 20 μL of DEPC-treated water.
Measure RNA concentration using the QuantiFluor RNA kit.
Index RNA using SMARTer Stranded Total RNA-Seq Kit v2 - Pico Input Mammalian following the manufacturer’s instructions with a fragmentation time of 3 min and 14 cycles of PCR amplification.
Sequence using 75-cycle paired end reads.
- To avoid RNA degradation thoroughly clean bench surfaces and pipettes with RNAseZAP. - Cell centrifugation speeds are optimised for erythroid progenitor cells and may need adjusting.

High-resolution targeted 3C interrogation of cis-regulatory element organization at genome-wide scale by Damien J. Downes, Robert A. Beagrie, Matthew E. Gosden, Jelena Telenius, Stephanie J. Carpenter, Lea Nussbaum, Sara De Ornellas, Martin Sergeant, Chris Q. Eijsbouts, Ron Schwessinger, Jon Kerry, Nigel Roberts, Arun Shivalingam, Afaf El-Sagheer, A. Marieke Oudelaar, Tom Brown, Veronica J. Buckle, James O. J. Davies, Jim R. Hughes published in Nature Communications on 22 Jan, 2021 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/s41467-020-20809-6
